title: Pressure sensing layers and devices comprising same
abstract: Pressure sensing layers, devices comprising same, pressure sensing monitors and composite materials comprising a) a porous matrix material comprising a siloxane polymer, comprising a closed porosity volume fraction, and, optionally, an open porosity volume fraction, and b) a carbonaceous conductive or semiconductive filler substantially present in said closed porosity volume fraction of said porous matrix material a), and films, coated substrates and multilayer structures comprising the composite material and the use thereof in pressure sensing devices.
claims: Claims Claims 1. Pressure sensing layer comprising a film comprising a) a first layer of porous matrix material comprising a siloxane polymer, comprising a closed porosity volume fraction, and, optionally, an open porosity volume fraction, and b) a carbonaceous conductive or semiconductive filler substantially present in said closed porosity volume fraction of said first layer of porous matrix material a) and, optionally c) one or more additional layers. 2. The pressure sensing layer in accordance with claim 1 wherein the ratio of closed porosity volume fraction to open porosity volume fraction is at least 1 :1 , preferably 1 :1 to 100:1 or wherein the closed porosity volume comprises 100% of the entire porosity volume. 3. The pressure sensing layer of any of claims claim 1 to 2 wherein the amount of filler is in the range of from 0.1 to 15 wt %, based on the entire weight of a) and b). 4. The pressure sensing layer of any of claims 1 to 3 wherein the siloxane polymer is a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS). 5. The pressure sensing layer of any of claims 1 to 4 wherein the conductive or semiconductive filler is selected from carbon nanotubes, carbon nanohorns, graphite, graphene and carbon black. 6. The pressure sensing layer of claim 5 wherein the conductive or semiconductive filler is carbon black. 7. The pressure sensing layer of any of claims 1 to 6 having a thickness in the range of from 0.1 to 500 pm. 8. The pressure sensing layer of any of claims 1 to 7 comprising a first layer and, adjacent thereto, a second layer which is an insulating layer. 9. The pressure sensing layer in accordance with claim 8 wherein the second layer is a polyester layer, particularly a polyethylene terephthalate layer. 10. The pressure sensing layer in accordance with any of claims 1 to 9 which is a sensing layer for sensing low pressures, preferably for sensing low pressures in the range from 0.01 to 100 kPa. 11. Substrate coated with a pressure sensing layer in accordance with any of claims 1 to 10. 12. The pressure sensing device comprising at least one electrode and at least one pressure sensing layer in accordance with any of claims 1 to 10. 13. The pressure sensing device in accordance with claim 12 providing a signal in response to a pressure variation wherein the pressure variation is a pulse wave event. 14. Pressure sensing monitor comprising a) at least one pressure sensing layer in accordance with any of claims 1 to 10 or a pressure sensing device in accordance with claims 12 or 13 providing a capacitance change signal in response to a pressure variation, b) a power supply, c) at least one signal converting unit capable of converting the capacitance change signal provided by the pressure sensing layer into an analog or digital electric signal, optionally with filters and amplifiers for the signal, d) at least one microcontroller unit capable of digitalizing an analog signal provided by signal converting unit c) and/or communicating digitally with the at least one signal converting unit c) and capable to convert the signal obtained from the at least one signal converting unit c) into other formats using suitable algorithms stored in the at least one controlling unit as readable code, and, optionally, e) at least one data transmission means for transmitting data provided by the microcontroller unit to a data receiving device, and/or f) means for communicating data provided by microcontroller unit d) or at least one data transmission means e) to a user. 15. The pressure sensing monitor in accordance with claim 14 wherein the pressure variation is a pulse wave event. 16. The pressure sensing monitor in accordance with claims 14 or 15 wherein the at least one controlling unit c) converts the signal obtained from the at least one signal converting unit b) into hemodynamic parameters. 17. The pressure sensing monitor in accordance with any of claims 14 to 16 which is a wearable device. 18. Composite material comprising a) a porous matrix material comprising a siloxane polymer, comprising a closed porosity volume fraction, and, optionally, an open porosity volume fraction, and b) a carbonaceous conductive or semiconductive filler substantially present in said closed porosity volume fraction of said porous matrix material a). 19. The composite material in accordance with claim 18 wherein the ratio of closed porosity volume fraction to open porosity volume fraction is at least 1 :1 , preferably 1 :1 to 100:1 or wherein the closed porosity volume comprises 100% of the entire porosity volume. 20. The composite material of any of claims claim 18 to 19 wherein the amount of filler is in the range of from 0.1 to 15 wt %, based on the entire weight of the composite. 21. The composite material of any of claims 18 to 20 wherein the siloxane polymer is a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS). 22. The composite material of any of claims 18 to 21 wherein the conductive or semiconductive filler is selected from carbon nanotubes, carbon nanohorns, graphite, graphene and carbon black, preferably carbon black. 23. Film comprising the composite material of any of claims 18 to 22. 24. The film of claim 23 having a thickness in the range of from 0.1 to 500 pm. 25. Multilayer system comprising a first layer of a film in accordance with any of claims 23 or 24 and, adjacent thereto, a second layer which is an insulating layer. 26. The multilayer system in accordance with claim 25 wherein the second layer is a polyester layer, particularly a polyethylene terephthalate layer. 27. Use of the composite materials in accordance with any of claims 18 to 22 or of the film in accordance with claim 23 or 24 or of the multilayer system in accordance with claim 25 or 26 in a pressure sensing device. 28. Use in accordance with claim 27 wherein the pressure sensing device is a blood pressure sensing device. 29. A process for the manufacture of a composite material in accordance with any of claims 18 to 22 comprising the following steps: a) providing a first non-aqueous phase comprising a siloxane polymer precursor and a curing agent and, optionally, a surfactant, b) providing a second aqueous phase comprising a conductive or semiconductive carbonaceous filler dispersed in water, c) preparing an emulsion by adding aqueous phase b) to the non-aqueous phase a) under stirring, d) reticulating the product obtained in step c) and , finally, d) subjecting the product obtained in step d) to a heat treatment to remove the water. 30. The process of claim 29 for manufacturing a film in accordance with claim 23 or 24 wherein the emulsion obtained in step c) is formed into a film by pouring same into a mold before applying step d).
